name: Southwest Strawberry Stack
recipeInstructions:
1. Place each tortilla on a 12-inch foil square.
2. Gently press each into a 9-inch pie pan.
3. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon cinnamon sugar over the bottom of each.
4. Fold the foil over the tortilla edges.
5. Bake at 450°F for 4 minutes. If the tortillas bubble, gently prick with a sharp fork.
6. Set aside. Reduce heat to 300°F.
7. In a large bowl, combine eggs, yogurt, sugar, lemon juice, and 1 teaspoon vanilla. Pour about 1-2/3 cups of mixture into each tortilla shell.
8. Bake for 20-30 minutes until set and the top loses its shine. Cool on wire racks.
9. Slice berries and set aside, reserving a few whole berries for garnish.
10. In a small saucepan, stir 1 tablespoon sugar and gelatin. Add water and stir over low heat until gelatin dissolves. Cool for 5 minutes.
11. Beat cream until it begins to thicken. Slowly beat in the gelatin mixture and remaining vanilla. Continue beating until the cream holds stiff peaks.
12. Carefully remove foil from one tortilla. Place on a serving plate and fill the center with about 1/3 of the whipped cream.
13. Arrange half sliced berries on top. Repeat with remaining tortillas.
14. Chill until ready to serve. Makes 8 servings.
